prompt: I'm launching a craft brewery in Bristol and need a website with e-commerce for merch, a taproom booking system, and a blog for beer culture content. I've got a logo and some brand colours but nothing else built yet. I want to set up an agent company for this project. I'm thinking maybe a design agent, a content agent, and a web dev agent but I'm not sure if that's right.
assertions: COMPANY.md exists at the sandbox root and contains an ownership map table with columns for Concern, Owner, and Notes
COMPANY.md contains a conflict resolution table addressing boundary edges between agents
COMPANY.md contains a named Sizing Rationale section defending the chosen number of agents
Each agent has a file at .claude/agents/{lowercase_name}.md with YAML frontmatter containing name, description, tools, model, and memory fields (path substitution OK for sandbox-blocked .claude/)
Each subagent frontmatter includes memory: project
Each subagent frontmatter description reads as a routing trigger (specifies when to invoke, mentions exclusions) — not a generic bio
Each subagent body contains 6 sections: Identity, Voice, Analytical Frameworks, Boundaries, Failure Modes, Standing Instructions
Each subagent's Boundaries section includes Owns, Does not own, and Hands off to subsections
No two agents claim ownership of the same concern in the ownership map
No agent-company/ folder is created in the sandbox (obsolete pattern)
Calibration transcripts, if persisted, live under .claude/agent-calibration/ or agent-calibration/ (never elsewhere)
The executor challenged the user's initial 3-agent suggestion rather than blindly accepting it
SEO or visibility is explicitly addressed — as its own agent, or assigned in the ownership map
An activation summary artefact exists (activation-summary.md or equivalent) reporting what was built, calibration results per agent, and routing sanity check result
The routing sanity check was run and its result (clean or flagged prompts) is reported in the activation summary
Each subagent Voice section includes at least 2 example sentences with specific, realistic content (not placeholder text)
Each subagent Analytical Frameworks section includes at least 2 named domain-specific frameworks with concrete criteria
At least one framework per agent names a domain-specific source (regulator, named methodology, official standard, published benchmark) — not a generic business tool
Each subagent Failure Modes section contains at least 3 role-specific failure modes (not generic advice)
Each subagent Failure Modes includes a failure mode about relying on training data instead of researching current sources
Each subagent Standing Instructions includes an instruction about verifying claims against current sources with URL citation
Each subagent Standing Instructions mentions consulting persistent memory at session start and saving learnings after significant tasks
Each subagent Standing Instructions references COMPANY.md at the project root for conflict resolution
The ownership map in COMPANY.md is consistent with the Owns sections in each subagent file — no discrepancies
Tools field is scoped per agent (writer agents lack Bash; research agents lack Edit unless justified)
